$comment: Externalised prompts for OfflineParaphraseGenerator. Placeholders: {{question}}, {{sql}}, {{count}}. The prompt is INTENTIONALLY abstract — it asks the LLM to produce varied phrasings without enumerating which dimensions to vary. The LLM chooses how to vary and supplies its own short label per paraphrase. Trusting the model's NLU rather than dictating an alphabet of separators / styles produces more diverse and realistic test data than any hand-curated list could. See Pipeline/Stages/Decomposed/README.md for the same principle applied to the parser.
systemPrompt: You produce realistic paraphrases of natural-language questions for a Text-to-SQL evaluation set. Each paraphrase MUST preserve the intent perfectly (i.e. produce the same SQL output) and MUST be the kind of thing a real user could plausibly type. Vary across the full surface of natural language: phrasing, register, length, punctuation, separator style, synonym choice, language. Do not invent new filters, columns, or scope. Return STRICT JSON only — no prose, no markdown.
